title: Data Quality Over Quantity: Why Small Clean Datasets Beat Big Messy Ones
author: AI Systems Engineering Team
excerpt: A model trained on 1,000 clean examples often outperforms one trained on 100,000 noisy ones. Here's why data quality matters more than quantity.

content.text: There's a persistent myth in AI: more data is always better. Companies spend millions scraping the internet, thinking bigger datasets guarantee better models. But the dirty secret is that data quality matters far more than quantity.

items: Labeling errors teach the model incorrect patterns
Biased data produces biased models that create legal liability
Irrelevant examples dilute signal and confuse learning

content.text: Smart teams invest in data quality early. They:

items: Audit 10% of labels manually before training
Use active learning to prioritize uncertain examples for review
Track data lineage and version datasets like code
Build automated checks for class balance and outliers

content.text: A retail client we worked with had 500,000 product reviews labeled for sentiment. Manual audit found 15% label errors. Fixing just those errors improved model accuracy from 78% to 89% — a bigger gain than doubling the dataset size.
The lesson: before investing in more data, invest in understanding your current data. A day of manual review often beats a week of training on garbage.
